name: Duty cycle exact lookup
tests: lookup_duty_cycle(process, voltage, amperage) tool + exact citation
axes: process in {MIG, TIG, Stick, Flux-cored}; voltage in {120V, 240V}; amperage within rated ranges; phrasing formal/casual/abbreviated
seeds: What's the duty cycle for MIG welding at 200A on 240V?
If I'm running flux-cored at 150 amps off 120V, how long can I weld before resting?
rubric: Must return the EXACT percentage from the table, cite owner-manual p.19, render calculator artifact. No computed numbers.

name: Polarity single-fact
tests: lookup_polarity(process) tool
axes: process; clamp terminology (ground/work clamp, stinger/electrode holder, torch); TIG special AC vs DCEN
seeds: What polarity do I use for 7018 stick electrode?
TIG on aluminum — DC or AC?
rubric: Correct polarity string (DCEN / DCEP / AC) + socket assignment, cited.

name: Spec single-fact
tests: retrieval over specs section
axes: weight, input voltage range, max amperage, wire size range, gas compatibility
seeds: What's the max amperage this machine can output?
How much does it weigh?
rubric: Exact value + page citation.

name: Polarity + socket assignment
tests: multi-section stitch (polarity rule + physical connection point)
axes: process; which clamp (ground vs torch vs stinger)
seeds: What polarity for TIG welding, and which socket does the ground clamp go in?
I want to run flux-cored — where do I plug the gun and the work clamp?
rubric: Polarity + correct socket label + SVG diagram + citations from both sections.

name: Symptom to diagnostic pathway
tests: troubleshooting_for(symptom, process) + procedural steps text
axes: defect in {porosity, spatter, burn-through, lack-of-fusion, undercut, bird-nesting, wandering arc, hard-starting, tungsten contamination}; process
seeds: I'm getting porosity in my flux-cored welds — what do I check?
My TIG arc keeps wandering. How do I fix it?
rubric: Ordered checklist + Mermaid flowchart artifact + citations to troubleshooting matrix page(s).

name: Process setup with safety trigger
tests: setup procedure + safety section cross-link
cross_refs: process setup + safety section (power-off warnings, PPE)
axes: action requiring power-off (changing polarity, swapping leads, opening wire compartment); process
seeds: How do I switch from MIG to stick?
Can I change the polarity while the machine is on?
rubric: Must include safety_flags entry (e.g., power_off_before_changing_leads) and PPE reminder when relevant.

name: Selection chart + owner manual cross-ref
tests: OCR-backed retrieval from image-only selection-chart.pdf + confirmation in owner manual
axes: material in {mild steel, stainless, aluminum}; thickness; process
seeds: What settings do I use for 1/8 inch mild steel with solid wire MIG?
I've got 1/16 inch aluminum — what process and settings?
rubric: Citations MUST include both doc_id: selection-chart AND doc_id: owner-manual. Missing selection chart = fail.

name: Quick-start + owner manual elaboration
tests: quick-start procedure + deeper owner-manual detail
axes: task in {cable install, first weld, wire loading, gas connection}
seeds: I just unboxed the machine — walk me through my first MIG weld.
The quick-start says to connect the work clamp — where exactly does it plug in?
rubric: Must pull procedure from quick-start AND elaborate detail from owner manual, both cited.

name: OCR-only fact
tests: facts that exist ONLY in image-based content (selection chart labels, diagram labels)
axes: chart cell values; diagram callouts; front-panel labels
seeds: What's the recommended wire speed for 3/16 inch steel per the selection chart?
What does the label on the front panel next to the main dial say?
rubric: Correct value with citation to the OCR-backed source; retrieval must not silently fall back to owner-manual native text.

name: Process abbreviation equivalence
tests: synonym map (TIG=GTAW, MIG=GMAW, flux-cored=FCAW, stick=SMAW)
axes: each abbreviation variant; mixed abbreviations in one question
seeds: What's the duty cycle for GTAW at 150A?
FCAW porosity fixes?
rubric: Same answer as colloquial variant. Synonym failure = fail.

name: Hardware synonym equivalence
tests: stinger=electrode holder, ground clamp=work clamp, gun=torch (MIG)
axes: hardware term; process
seeds: Where does the stinger plug in for 7018?
Which socket is the work clamp for TIG?
rubric: Correctly maps to the manual's canonical terminology and answers with citation.

name: Voltage phrasing equivalence
tests: 120 volt equals 120V equals standard outlet equals dual voltage low side
cross_refs: input voltage spec + duty cycle table
axes: voltage phrasing variant
seeds: Can I run this off a standard household outlet?
Running on dual voltage low side — what changes in duty cycle?
rubric: Resolves to 120V branch of tables; if ambiguous, asks for clarification.

name: Missing required parameter
tests: agent detects unspecified axis needed for table lookup
axes: missing process / missing voltage / missing amperage / missing material
seeds: What's the duty cycle?
What polarity do I need?
rubric: Must ask a targeted clarifying question naming the missing axis. Guessing a default = fail.

name: Multi-valid-interpretation
tests: question maps to multiple equally valid answers
axes: weld scenario with multiple valid processes
seeds: What's the best way to weld aluminum?
rubric: Enumerates the valid paths and asks which the user wants, OR presents both with tradeoffs + citations.

name: Underspecified material/thickness
tests: selection-chart lookup requires material AND thickness
axes: missing thickness / missing material subtype
seeds: What settings for steel?
rubric: Asks for thickness (and steel type if relevant).

name: Out-of-range parameter
tests: parameter exceeds machine spec
axes: amperage above max; voltage outside input range; duty cycle claim at impossible amp
seeds: What's the duty cycle for MIG at 300A on 240V?
Can I run this on 480V 3-phase?
rubric: Identifies out-of-range premise, cites the spec, refuses to fabricate a value.

name: Impossible process/parameter combo
tests: catches physically invalid combos
axes: invalid polarity/process pair; invalid gas/wire pair
seeds: What AC amperage for MIG on aluminum?
Which flux-cored wire do I use with gas shielding and DCEN polarity?
rubric: Flags the invalid combo with a manual citation explaining the constraint.

name: Wrong product claim
tests: agent doesn't hallucinate about a machine that doesn't exist
axes: fake model number; fake feature from another machine
seeds: On my Vulcan OmniPro 300, is the duty cycle the same?
Does this model have the auto-set feature from the 180?
rubric: Clarifies scope (this corpus covers OmniPro 220 only) and does not invent cross-model facts.

name: Citation forging bait
tests: when asked about a fact not in the manual, refuses to manufacture a page reference
axes: fact clearly absent from welding corpus
seeds: What page covers using this for underwater welding?
rubric: Admits absence; does NOT return a fake citation.

name: SVG diagram required
tests: spatial / wiring / polarity triggers svg_diagram
axes: process; request phrasing (show/draw/diagram)
seeds: Show me how to wire up for DCEN stick welding.
rubric: Valid sanitizable SVG with labeled terminals + machine outline.

name: HTML calculator required
tests: parameter-to-numeric-output triggers html_widget
axes: widget subject (duty cycle, settings configurator)
seeds: Give me a duty cycle calculator I can play with.
rubric: Interactive sandbox widget with process/voltage/amperage inputs and deterministic output from the tool.

name: Mermaid flowchart required
tests: decision tree triggers flowchart artifact
axes: defect type
seeds: Help me troubleshoot weld bead that's too wide and flat.
rubric: Mermaid decision tree tied to troubleshooting entries with leaf nodes citing pages.

name: Manual image surfacing
tests: show-me phrasing triggers manual_image artifact
axes: image target (front panel, polarity diagram, wire feed, inside view)
seeds: Show me the front panel layout.
What does the correct polarity diagram look like?
rubric: Returns correct image path with page + figure caption citation.

name: Multi-artifact response
tests: agent emits 2+ artifacts in one response when justified
axes: combined process setup + parameter calculation
seeds: Set me up for TIG welding — I want to see the polarity and also calculate my duty cycle at 120A.
rubric: Response contains both svg_diagram and html_widget; citations cover both.

name: Settings panel photo
tests: image-type classification to panel; OCR on labels; match to manual panel page; compare configured vs intended
axes: intended process/material; panel state (clearly set, partially set, wrong set)
seeds: [image: front panel close-up] Is this set up right for TIG on thin aluminum?
rubric: States what machine appears set to, flags uncertainty, compares to recommended setup from selection chart, cites manual panel page.

name: Cable/socket photo
tests: image-type to setup; spatial reasoning; polarity inference from visible cables
axes: process user intends; cable visibility
seeds: [image: cable hookup] Did I wire this up correctly for stick welding?
rubric: Identifies which cable is where, compares to expected polarity, asks for a better angle if occluded.

name: Weld bead photo
tests: visual defect reasoning; retrieval of defect-matching troubleshooting page; no OCR dependence
axes: defect pattern visible in image
seeds: [image: weld bead] What's wrong with this bead?
rubric: Names likely defect with confidence level, cites troubleshooting page, requests settings photo if a real diagnosis needs it.

name: General equipment photo
tests: part identification against product images and figure callouts
axes: component subject
seeds: [image: machine component] What part of the machine is this?
rubric: Identifies component, cites the figure it matches, declines confidently if unmatched.

name: Insufficient-image follow-up
tests: agent asks for better photos instead of guessing
axes: image quality issue (blurry, wrong angle, too far)
seeds: [image: blurry close-up] Is this bead okay?
rubric: Describes what can and cannot be seen, asks for specific follow-up shot. No overconfident diagnosis.

name: First-weld walkthrough
tests: unboxing to cable install to wire/electrode load to polarity to test weld
cross_refs: quick-start-guide.pdf + owner-manual polarity + owner-manual process setup
axes: process (MIG, TIG, stick, flux-cored); material
seeds: I just got the machine — walk me through making my first MIG weld on mild steel.
rubric: Ordered steps with citations from 2+ PDFs; safety_flags at polarity/power-on steps; artifact either SVG of setup or flowchart of procedure.

name: Process changeover
tests: sequence of actions to switch processes (physical + setting + polarity + consumable)
axes: from-process to to-process pair
seeds: I was running MIG — how do I change over to stick?
rubric: Complete checklist + power-off safety flag + consumable swap notes + citations.

name: Consumable change procedure
tests: wire change / tip change / tungsten regrind procedure
cross_refs: wire feed / tip / tungsten sections of owner manual
axes: consumable type; size change
seeds: How do I change from 0.030 to 0.035 MIG wire?
rubric: Step list with tensioner/drive-roll references, cited.

name: Follow-up with pronoun/ellipsis
tests: carries context from prior turn
axes: implicit parameter being changed
seeds: Prior: Duty cycle for MIG at 200A / 240V? // Now: And at 150A?
rubric: Resolves implicit process+voltage from context; answers correctly. (Question should be a single string noting prior-turn context.)

name: Within-session contradiction
tests: user asserts wrong fact; agent corrects with citation
axes: false premise embedded in phrasing
seeds: I'm running MIG on AC — what duty cycle at 200A?
rubric: Corrects the AC premise (MIG is DCEP) and cites; does not answer as if the premise were valid.

name: Off-topic / out-of-scope
tests: declines politely without fabricating
axes: unrelated topic
seeds: What's the weather in SF today?
Can I use this welder to heat my garage?
rubric: Graceful redirect to corpus scope; no hallucinated answer.

name: Hostile phrasing / long rambling context
tests: information extraction from messy input
axes: noise length; real question location in input
seeds: long 150-word story ending with 'so, basically, what polarity for TIG?'
rubric: Extracts the real question and answers it.
